JPM.reasoning: Catalyst window: No identifiable catalyst within 10 trading days. Q1 2026 earnings occurred April 14 (already priced). FOMC April 28-29 just concluded with no rate change (as expected, neutral). Q2 2026 earnings are ~55 trading days away (mid-July). The catalyst-driven entry condition is not met. Net news sentiment: mixed. Q1 beat was strong (+9% EPS beat, +18% IB fees, record equities trading) but the counter-catalyst — NII guidance trimmed from $104.5B to ~$103B and Dimon's cautious shareholder letter — arrived simultaneously, creating a ceiling. Morgan Stanley cut target on NII trim; Goldman raised to $365. No fresh material headline in the past week. Insider activity: wiki documents multiple board director buys in March 2026 (positive corroborating signal), but the live bundle shows no recent insider trades — quiet currently. Technical setup: JPM ran from ~$280 to $320.24 post-earnings, now pulling back to $309.25. Short-term structure is weak — price below 5/10 EMA/SMA on daily, below all short-term MAs on hourly, hourly MACD negative, hourly OBV trending down, hourly volume bias 10d is down. Elevated hourly relative volume (2.1x) skews bearish (distribution). RSI-7 at 23.27 suggests short-term oversold, but the lack of a catalyst means this is not an actionable bounce setup for catalyst-news strategy. Key support at $307.29 (volume-confirmed, 13 hourly tests) and $304.26 (13 daily tests). Resistance at $314.99 (22 hourly tests). The stock is in a constructive digestion after a strong earnings run, but without a near-term catalyst, the catalyst-news strategy mandate requires neutral. Analyst consensus remains Moderate Buy with average target $337, providing a bullish backdrop for when Q2 earnings approach.
